mission.title: Install and Configure Kubevirt on Kubernetes
mission.description: KubeVirt is a virtual machine management add-on for Kubernetes that allows you to define and manage virtual machines using Kubernetes' Custom Resource Definitions. Installing KubeVirt enables you to run virtual machines alongside your container workloads in a Kubernetes cluster.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the KubeVirt Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add kubevirt https://encircle360-oss.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install KubeVirt using Helm
steps.description:
Install KubeVirt in the 'kubevirt' namespace with the specified version:
```bash
helm install kubevirt kubevirt/kubevirt --namespace kubevirt --create-namespace --version 0.2.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the KubeVirt components are running correctly:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubevirt
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the KubeVirt components by applying the following configuration:
```bash
kubectl apply -f examples/example-migration-policy.yaml
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the KubeVirt release from your cluster:
```bash
helm uninstall kubevirt --namespace kubevirt
```

uninstall.title: Clean up Custom Resource Definitions
uninstall.description:
Delete the KubeVirt CRDs to remove any remaining resources:
```bash
kubectl delete crd virtualmachines.kubevirt.io virtualmachineinstances.kubevirt.io migrationpolicies.migrations.kubevirt.io --namespace kubevirt
```

uninstall.title: Remove the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'kubevirt' namespace to clean up all resources:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace kubevirt
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Export the current configuration and resources before upgrading:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubevirt -o yaml > kubevirt-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure you have the latest Helm chart:
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade KubeVirt
upgrade.description:
Upgrade KubeVirt to the latest version:
```bash
helm upgrade kubevirt kubevirt/kubevirt --namespace kubevirt --version 0.2.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the KubeVirt components are still running correctly after the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubevirt
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If KubeVirt pods are stuck in a Pending state, check for resource availability:
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n kubevirt
``` 
Ensure that your cluster has enough resources (CPU, memory) to schedule the pods.

troubleshooting.title: Virtual machines not starting
troubleshooting.description:
If virtual machines fail to start, check the logs of the KubeVirt controller:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app=kubevirt-controller -n kubevirt
``` 
Look for any error messages that may indicate configuration issues.

troubleshooting.title: Migration failures
troubleshooting.description:
If VM migrations are failing, check the migration policy configuration:
```bash
kubectl get migrationpolicy -n kubevirt
``` 
Ensure that the policy allows for the intended migration actions.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of KubeVirt will result in all KubeVirt components running in the 'kubevirt' namespace, and you will be able to create and manage virtual machines using Kubernetes resources. You can verify the installation by checking the status of the pods and ensuring they are in a Running state.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add kubevirt https://encircle360-oss.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, along with Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
